article_title: Selinexor Plus Ruxolitinib Shrinks Spleens but Misses Its Symptom Goal in Phase 3 SENTRY, With an Early Survival Signal in Myelofibrosis
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: Selinexor Plus Ruxolitinib Shrinks Spleens but Misses Its Symptom Goal in Phase 3 SENTRY, With an Early Survival Signal in Myelofibrosis
content_preview.summary: The SENTRY trial of selinexor plus ruxolitinib met its spleen-volume endpoint and missed its symptom endpoint, with an immature overall survival signal favoring the combination.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A Phase 3 trial of selinexor added to ruxolitinib in patients with myelofibrosis hit its spleen-shrinkage goal but missed its symptom goal, leaving the combination with a mixed primary result and an early, still-immature signal that it may extend survival. The SENTRY trial was presented as a late-breaking oral presentation at the 2026 American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) Annual Meeting on June 2, 2026, with simultaneous publication in the Journal of Clinical Oncology, [Karyo...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).

editor_notes.content_quality: Clear, well-structured News piece with appropriate Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis framing for a clinical-trial result. Technical depth (co-primary endpoints, SVR35, Abs-TSS, hazard ratio, confidence interval, immaturity of OS data) is handled accurately and accessibly. 898 words, within the News 400-1200 range.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0.html.gz (Karyopharm/PR Newswire, ASCO, status 200): confirmed SVR35 49.8% vs 28.0% at week 24, odds ratio 2.58 (95% CI 1.60-4.17; p<0.0001); Abs-TSS adjusted mean difference 0.97 (95% CI -1.07 to 3.02; p=0.825) co-primary NOT met; OS HR 0.43 (95% CI 0.19-1.00; nominal one-sided p=0.022); 224/235 (95.3%) vs 106/118 (89.8%) alive as of February 20, 2026; N=353, randomized 2-to-1, 60 mg once-weekly, JAK-inhibitor-naive, platelets >100x10^9/L; XPORT-MF-034 / NCT04562389; TEAEs 99.1% vs 97.4%; thrombocytopenia 59% vs 43%, nausea 57% vs 17%, neutropenia 27% vs 9%; grade 3+ 70% vs 50%; TEAEs leading to death 0.9% vs 2.6%; confirmed leukemic transformation 1.7% each arm; June 2, 2026 late-breaking oral presentation; Mascarenhas quote and title verbatim. source-1.html.gz (Menarini Group/PR Newswire, EHA, status 200): confirmed arm sizes n=235 / n=118, SVR35 week 12 (49.4% vs 20.3%), week 24 (49.8% vs 28.0%), week 36 (46.9% vs 23.0%); symptom improvement 9.9 vs 10.9 points; HR 0.43, 'greater than 50% reduction in the risk of death'; 'No new safety signals were observed' verbatim; Harrison quote ('Achievement of spleen reduction is the essential goal of myelofibrosis treatment') and title verbatim; Ergun quote ('the strength of the spleen response and the encouraging early overall survival data observed in the SENTRY study creates hope for a potential new treatment option for patients suffering from this devastating disease with dismal outcomes') and title verbatim. source-2.html.gz (StockTitan, status 200): independently confirms the co-primary split — 'SENTRY met SVR35 endpoint with strong HR but missed symptom endpoint', 'hazard ratio of 0.43, but the symptom endpoint was not met', co-primary endpoints SVR35 and Abs-TSS over 24 weeks. Rule 9 check: the JCO primary publication is bot-blocked/not allowlisted and was correctly dropped from the source list; every numeric specific, quote, name, and date in the article is independently carried by the three accessible sources above and NONE rests on the JCO.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims align with the cited accessible sources. The split co-primary result is reported correctly: the trial MET the spleen-volume co-primary (SVR35) and MISSED the symptom co-primary (Abs-TSS, p=0.825). The overall survival signal (HR 0.43) is correctly framed throughout as a pre-specified secondary endpoint that is immature/suggestive rather than confirmatory, with the article explicitly noting the CI reaches up to 1.00 and the p-value is nominal and one-sided. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The automated linter flagged the word 'clearly' in the Analysis sentence 'the combination clearly outperformed ruxolitinib on spleen shrinkage'. The characterization is factually defensible given a p<0.0001 result, but the word is mildly emphatic editorial phrasing; documented as a clarification in the corrections record rather than treated as a factual error.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No prior Machine Herald coverage of selinexor, ruxolitinib, myelofibrosis, or the SENTRY trial. Sits alongside other June 2026 Biotech & Medicine coverage (ASCO/EHA readouts) but is a distinct story.
editor_notes.allowlist_note: prnewswire.com and stocktitan.net snapshots fetched successfully (status 200). No blocklisted domains. Any allowlist coverage warnings are non-blocking and were not acted on; the allowlist file was not modified.
recommendations: Filed an APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS clarification noting the single mildly emphatic word 'clearly' in the Analysis section; the underlying statement (49.8% vs 28.0%, p<0.0001) is accurate.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-sourced, neutral clinical-trial report. Every specific is independently verified against the three accessible sources and none depends on the bot-blocked JCO publication. The split co-primary outcome and the immature OS signal are reported correctly. Approving with a minor clarification corrections record for the lone loaded-language warning.
